disqualifications: Corrupt practice or electoral offense conviction

votingMethods.name: In-Person Voting
votingMethods.description: Vote at your designated polling booth using your Voter ID / Aadhaar
steps: Find your polling booth via ECI portal or Voter Helpline 1950
Carry valid photo ID (Voter ID, Aadhaar, Passport, PAN card)
Join queue and receive ballot / EVM slip
Mark your choice on the EVM
Get Indelible ink mark on finger

votingMethods.name: Postal Ballot
votingMethods.description: Available for service voters, seniors (85+), PwD, and essential services
steps: Submit Form 12D to RO 5 days before notification
Receive postal ballot at registered address
Fill in vote and seal envelope
Return to Returning Officer before counting day

votingMethods.name: Electronic Voting Machine
votingMethods.description: India's primary voting system — tamper-proof EVM with VVPAT
steps: Press button next to your candidate's name/symbol

registrationSteps.title: Check Eligibility
registrationSteps.description: Ensure you are 18+ and an Indian citizen residing in the constituency

registrationSteps.title: Fill Form 6
registrationSteps.description: Apply online on Voter Helpline app or voters.eci.gov.in for new registration

registrationSteps.title: Upload Documents
registrationSteps.description: Age proof (birth cert / school certificate), address proof (Aadhaar / utility bill), passport photo

registrationSteps.title: Submit Application
registrationSteps.description: Submit online or at your local BLO / ERO office. Get acknowledgment slip.

registrationSteps.title: BLO Verification
registrationSteps.description: Booth Level Officer visits for field verification within 30 days

registrationSteps.title: Receive Voter ID
registrationSteps.description: EPIC (Voter ID card) issued by ERO. Can download e-EPIC from Voter Helpline app.

timeline.label: Election Announcement
timeline.description: ECI announces election schedule — Model Code of Conduct kicks in

timeline.label: Voter Roll Freeze
timeline.description: Last date to register or update Voter ID before publication of final rolls

timeline.label: Candidate Nominations
timeline.description: Candidates file nomination papers with Returning Officer

timeline.label: Campaign Period
timeline.description: Active campaigning by parties and candidates

timeline.label: Polling Day
timeline.description: Cast your vote at your designated polling station (7 AM – 6 PM)

timeline.label: Vote Counting
timeline.description: EVM votes counted at counting centers. Results announced.

timeline.label: Official Results
timeline.description: ECI declares winners. Winners take oath of office.

faqs.q: What ID can I use to vote if I lost my Voter ID?
faqs.a: You can use 12 alternative IDs: Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, PAN, MNREGA Job Card, Service Identity Card, Passbooks with photo, Health Smart Card, Pension Document, NPR Smart Card, or any other photo ID issued by government.

faqs.q: Can I vote if I'm away from my constituency?
faqs.a: You must vote in the constituency where you are registered. Domestic migrants can register at current address using Form 6. Overseas voters can apply under ROVA scheme.
NOTA (None of the Above) is an option on the EVM that allows voters to reject all candidates. It is represented by the last button on the EVM but does not affect election results.
No. Voting is a right, not a legal obligation at the national level. However, some states have compulsory voting laws at local body elections.

faqs.q: How long does voter registration take?
faqs.a: Online applications are processed in 4–6 weeks. The BLO verifies within 30 days. You can download e-EPIC immediately on successful verification.
